# Data Model: trc.chain

**Feature**: TRI-27-trc-chain-orchestrator
**Date**: 2026-04-15

This feature is primarily agent orchestration, not data management. Only three entities have persistent on-disk representation. Ephemeral entities (from `spec.md` ‚Üí Key Entities) are documented for completeness but are in-memory only.

---

## Persistent Entities

### 1. Chain Run State (`state.json`)

**Location**: `specs/.chain-runs/<run-id>/state.json`

**Run ID format**: `<ISO8601-compact>-<first-ticket-id>`, e.g. `20260415T123456-TRI-100`. Generated by `chain-run.sh init`. Unique per invocation.

**Schema**:

```json
{
  "run_id": "20260415T123456-TRI-100",
  "created_at": "2026-04-15T12:34:56Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-04-15T12:45:00Z",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "terminal_reason": null,
  "ticket_ids": ["TRI-100", "TRI-101", "TRI-102"],
  "current_index": 1,
  "epic_brief_path": "specs/.chain-runs/20260415T123456-TRI-100/epic-brief.md",
  "tickets": {
    "TRI-100": {
      "status": "completed",
      "branch": "TRI-100-user-auth",
      "worktree_path": "../tricycle-pro-TRI-100-user-auth",
      "pr_url": "https://github.com/org/repo/pull/123",
      "lint_status": "pass",
      "test_status": "pass",
      "report_path": "specs/.chain-runs/20260415T123456-TRI-100/TRI-100.report.md",
      "started_at": "2026-04-15T12:34:56Z",
      "finished_at": "2026-04-15T12:44:30Z",
      "open_questions": []
    },
    "TRI-101": {
      "status": "in_progress",
      "branch": "TRI-101-profile-page",
      "worktree_path": "../tricycle-pro-TRI-101-profile-page",
      "pr_url": null,
      "lint_status": null,
      "test_status": null,
      "report_path": null,
      "started_at": "2026-04-15T12:44:30Z",
      "finished_at": null,
      "open_questions": []
    },
    "TRI-102": {
      "status": "not_started",
      "branch": null,
      "worktree_path": null,
      "pr_url": null,
      "lint_status": null,
      "test_status": null,
      "report_path": null,
      "started_at": null,
      "finished_at": null,
      "open_questions": []
    }
  }
}
```

**Fields**:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `run_id` | string | yes | Unique run identifier. Immutable after `init`. |
| `created_at` | ISO8601 string | yes | Run creation timestamp. Immutable. |
| `updated_at` | ISO8601 string | yes | Last state transition timestamp. Updated on every write. |
| `status` | enum | yes | One of `in_progress`, `completed`, `failed`, `aborted`. See "Chain Run Lifecycle". |
| `terminal_reason` | string \| null | no | Human-readable reason if `status` is `failed` or `aborted`. Null while `in_progress` or `completed`. |
| `ticket_ids` | string[] | yes | Ordered list of ticket IDs in this run. Length 1‚Äì8. Deduplicated at init. |
| `current_index` | integer | yes | Index into `ticket_ids` of the ticket currently being processed (or next to process if all prior are complete). `0` when starting. Equals `len(ticket_ids)` when all done. |
| `epic_brief_path` | string \| null | no | Filesystem path (relative to repo root) to the shared epic brief, or null if none. |
| `tickets` | object | yes | Per-ticket state keyed by ticket ID. See "Ticket Entry". |

**Ticket Entry fields**:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `status` | enum | yes | One of `not_started`, `in_progress`, `completed`, `failed`, `skipped`. |
| `branch` | string \| null | no | Git branch name assigned by the worker (from `create-new-feature.sh`). |
| `worktree_path` | string \| null | no | Absolute or repo-relative path to the worker's worktree. |
| `pr_url` | string \| null | no | GitHub PR URL if push was approved and succeeded; null otherwise. |
| `lint_status` | enum \| null | no | `pass`, `fail`, or `skipped` (if project has no lint configured). |
| `test_status` | enum \| null | no | `pass`, `fail`, or `skipped`. |
| `report_path` | string \| null | no | Path to the worker's final structured report markdown file. |
| `started_at` | ISO8601 \| null | no | Worker spawn timestamp. |
| `finished_at` | ISO8601 \| null | no | Worker final-return timestamp. |
| `open_questions` | string[] | yes | List of any user-facing caveats or open questions the worker flagged. Defaults to empty array. |

**Validation rules** (enforced by `chain-run.sh` subcommands, not just by convention):

- `ticket_ids.length` MUST be between 1 and 8 inclusive at `init` (FR-003). `update-ticket` cannot change this.
- Every key in `tickets` MUST appear in `ticket_ids`, and vice versa, at every write.
- `current_index` MUST be in `[0, ticket_ids.length]`.
- `status == "in_progress"` implies at most one ticket has ticket-level `status == "in_progress"`.
- `status == "completed"` implies every ticket has ticket-level `status in {completed, skipped}`.
- `status == "failed"` implies at least one ticket has ticket-level `status == "failed"` AND `terminal_reason` is non-null.
- Tickets with `pr_url != null` MUST have `status == "completed"` and both `lint_status` and `test_status` equal `"pass"` (reflects FR-008 quality gates + FR-009 push approval happened).
- Timestamps are monotonic: `updated_at >= created_at`, `finished_at >= started_at`.

### 2. Epic Brief (`epic-brief.md`)

**Location**: `specs/.chain-runs/<run-id>/epic-brief.md` (from clarification Q2)

**Schema**: None. Free-form markdown. The orchestrator passes its **path** (not contents) to every worker. Workers read it themselves.

**Lifecycle**: Created at `init` time only if the user provided one or accepted the offer to create one. Never modified by the orchestrator mid-run. Never deleted by `close` ‚Äî it remains alongside the closed `state.json` for historical reference.

### 3. Progress Event (`<ticket-id>.progress`)

**Location**: `specs/.chain-runs/<run-id>/<ticket-id>.progress`

**Schema**: Single-line JSON, overwritten per transition:

```json
{"phase": "plan", "started_at": "2026-04-15T12:34:56Z", "ticket_id": "TRI-100"}
```

**Fields**:

| Field | Type | Required | Allowed values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `phase` | string | yes | `specify`, `clarify`, `plan`, `tasks`, `analyze`, `implement`, `push`, or `done` |
| `started_at` | ISO8601 string | yes | Timestamp of phase entry |
| `ticket_id` | string | yes | Must match the filename segment |

**Lifecycle**: Written by the worker at each phase transition (per FR-023). Read by the orchestrator for FR-022 display. Not persisted after run closes ‚Äî `chain-run.sh close` deletes `.progress` files but leaves `state.json` and reports intact.

---

## Chain Run Lifecycle

```text
(nothing)
    ‚îÇ
    ‚îÇ chain-run.sh init  (ticket list parsed, run-id generated)
    ‚ñº
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ   in_progress    ‚îÇ‚óÑ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò                  ‚îÇ
         ‚îÇ                            ‚îÇ orchestrator resumes after
         ‚îÇ update-ticket...           ‚îÇ a previous session exit
         ‚îÇ (per-ticket transitions)   ‚îÇ
         ‚îÇ                            ‚îÇ
         ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê  ‚îÇ
         ‚îÇ                         ‚îÇ  ‚îÇ
         ‚îÇ all tickets             ‚îÇ  ‚îÇ mid-run terminal failure
         ‚îÇ completed               ‚îÇ  ‚îÇ (FR-012) or user abort
         ‚ñº                         ‚ñº  ‚îÇ
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê     ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ    completed     ‚îÇ     ‚îÇ  failed / aborted ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò     ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
         ‚îÇ                         ‚îÇ
         ‚îÇ chain-run.sh close      ‚îÇ chain-run.sh close
         ‚ñº                         ‚ñº
   (terminal, not                (terminal, not
    offered for resume)           offered for resume)
```

**Rules**:
- Only `in_progress` runs are surfaced by `list-interrupted` (FR-020).
- `close` is idempotent: calling it on an already-closed run is a no-op with a diagnostic warning.
- `in_progress` ‚Üí `in_progress` transitions happen on every `update-ticket`; the top-level status stays the same but `updated_at` advances.
- A run is considered "interrupted" if `status == "in_progress"` and `updated_at` is older than some threshold (default: any, surfaced unconditionally). The orchestrator decides based on user intent what to offer.

---

## Ephemeral Entities (in-memory only, no schema)

- **Ticket** ‚Äî Linear issue (`id`, `title`, `body`). Fetched once at chain start (FR-002), held in orchestrator memory, embedded into each worker's prompt. Never persisted to disk (deliberate: keeps the orchestrator's context hygiene simple).
- **Worker Agent** ‚Äî The Claude Code sub-agent spawned per ticket. Identified by `chain-worker-<ticket-id>` name. Lives only for the duration of its ticket's trc run. Disappears when it returns.
- **Worker Report** ‚Äî The <300-word structured message each worker returns on completion. Its contents are destructured into the `tickets.<id>` fields of `state.json` (branch, pr_url, lint_status, test_status, open_questions) and optionally written to a `report_path` markdown file for the summary table. The raw text is not kept in orchestrator context beyond the destructuring step (per FR-014).
- **Worktree** ‚Äî Git working directory per ticket. Managed by `create-new-feature.sh --provision-worktree` (from TRI-26), referenced by `tickets.<id>.worktree_path`. Cleanup is handled by the worker's implement phase (existing `worktree-cleanup` block from TRI-22), not by the orchestrator.
